# Obsigna

> Tooling for the Agent Receipts protocol

Obsigna is the open-source toolset for producing and verifying Agent Receipts — cryptographically signed, hash-chained records of AI agent actions. It provides SDKs, an MCP proxy, a PostToolUse hook, a collector daemon, and a dashboard.

## Key links

- Getting Started: https://obsigna.dev/getting-started/quick-start/
- MCP Proxy: https://obsigna.dev/mcp-proxy/overview/
- Hook: https://obsigna.dev/hook/overview/
- Dashboard: https://obsigna.dev/dashboard/overview/
- Protocol specification: https://agentreceipts.ai/specification/overview/
- GitHub: https://github.com/agent-receipts/obsigna

## Components

- mcp-proxy: MCP STDIO proxy that audits tool calls, applies policy, and emits receipts
- hook: PostToolUse hook for Claude Code and other agent runtimes
- daemon: Out-of-agent collector that signs and stores receipts
- dashboard: Read UI for activity timelines, error rates, and per-tool breakdowns
- sdk-go: Go SDK for creating and verifying receipts
- sdk-ts: TypeScript SDK (@obsigna/sdk-ts)
- sdk-py: Python SDK (obsigna)
- openclaw: OpenClaw plugin for automatic receipt generation

## Core concepts

- Audit boundary: Receipts are signed outside the agent process, so an agent cannot forge its own audit trail
- Agent Receipt: A signed record of a single agent action (W3C Verifiable Credential, Ed25519, SHA-256)
- Receipt Chain: Hash-linked sequence of receipts forming a tamper-evident log
- Policy: Rules the MCP proxy enforces (allow, deny, require approval) before a tool call runs
